items.note: No visa required for citizens. ESTA required for Visa Waiver Program countries.
Major carriers: AT&T, T-Mobile, Verizon. Prepaid SIMs available at airports and convenience stores.
Public WiFi widely available but often unsecured. Always use VPN on hotel and café networks.
Common: fake charity collectors, ticket scalpers near tourist sites, rigged taxi meters in some cities.
Encryption tools are legal. Photography restrictions vary by state and venue. CBP can inspect devices at the border.
eTA required for visa-exempt foreign nationals flying into Canada. Not required for US citizens.
Major carriers: Rogers, Bell, Telus. Canada has some of the highest mobile data costs in the world — buy a prepaid plan.
Low risk. Public WiFi common and generally reliable. Use VPN on shared networks as standard practice.
Common: overpayment scams, fake rental listings in major cities, grandparent scams targeting tourists.
CBSA officers can demand device passwords at the border. Encryption tools are fully legal.
No visa required for most nationalities for stays under 180 days. Keep your FMM tourist card — you need it to exit.
Telcel has the best national coverage. Prepaid SIMs available everywhere. Data is affordable.
Medium risk in tourist areas. Avoid ATMs in non-bank locations — skimming is common. Use VPN on all public networks.
Common: taxi overcharging (use Uber or InDriver), fake police asking for bribes, timeshare hard-sells.
VPN use is legal. Carrying large amounts of cash can attract scrutiny. Photography of military installations is prohibited.
No visa required for most nationalities for up to 90 days. Onward ticket may be requested at entry.
Kolbi, Claro, and Movistar all offer prepaid SIMs. Coverage can be spotty in remote areas.
Low to medium. Phishing and card skimming have increased. Use contactless payments where possible.
Common: taxi overcharging, fake tour operators, distraction theft in crowded markets.
VPN use is legal. Costa Rica is considered one of the safer Central American destinations for nomads.
No visa required for most nationalities for up to 180 days. Proof of onward travel often requested.
Claro and Digicel are the main carriers. Prepaid SIMs cheap and widely available.
Low to medium. Panama City has solid infrastructure. Use VPN on hotel networks as standard.
Common: taxi overcharging in Panama City, currency confusion with USD (Panama uses USD).
Panama Friendly Nations Visa and digital nomad visa options available for longer stays. VPN use is legal.
Visa-free for most nationalities up to 6 months. ETA required from 2025 for many countries.
Three, EE, Vodafone, O2. Prepaid SIMs cheap — Three offers good international data plans.
Low risk. One of the most surveilled countries in the world via CCTV — not a cyber risk but worth knowing.
Common: ticket machine distraction theft, fake Wi-Fi hotspots in tourist areas, overpriced currency exchanges.
Investigatory Powers Act gives broad surveillance authority. Encryption is legal but authorities can compel decryption keys.
Schengen zone — 90 days in any 180-day period for most non-EU nationalities. Digital Nomad Visa available for longer stays.
NOS, MEO, Vodafone Portugal. Excellent 4G/5G coverage. Affordable prepaid SIMs at the airport.
Low risk. Lisbon and Porto have strong digital infrastructure. Public WiFi widely available.
Common: overpriced restaurants near tourist sites, fake ticket sellers for trams and attractions.
One of the most nomad-friendly countries in Europe. VPN use is fully legal. NHR tax regime may benefit long-term stays.
Schengen zone. Digital Nomad Visa available since 2023 for non-EU remote workers.
Movistar, Orange, Vodafone Spain. Prepaid SIMs affordable. Excellent urban coverage.
Low risk. Pickpocketing is common in Barcelona — secure your devices physically.
Common: Barcelona card game scams, fake petitioners, distraction theft on La Rambla.
VPN use is legal. Some geo-blocked content. Anti-protest laws restrict some public photography.
Schengen zone. Freelance visa available for non-EU workers planning to stay longer.
Telekom, Vodafone, O2. Coverage can be patchy in rural areas — Telekom has the best reach.
Low risk. Strong data protection culture (GDPR origin). Public WiFi less prevalent than other EU countries.
Common: fake charity workers, card skimming at tourist ATMs, overpriced currency exchange kiosks.
Strict privacy laws — good for nomads. VPN use is legal. Some content geo-blocked via GEMA.
Schengen zone. Talent Passport visa available for skilled remote workers.
Orange, SFR, Bouygues, Free Mobile. Free Mobile offers excellent value prepaid.
Low risk. Be cautious on Paris metro public WiFi — use VPN.
Common: gold ring scam near the Eiffel Tower, petition scams, distraction pickpocketing on the metro.
VPN use is legal. HADOPI anti-piracy law monitors torrenting. Photography of people requires consent in some contexts.
Visa-free for 1 year for most nationalities including US, EU, UK. One of the most open entry policies in the world.
Magti, Geocell (Silknet), Beeline. Cheap and fast. Tbilisi has excellent connectivity.
Low to medium. Growing nomad infrastructure. Use VPN as standard — some Russian services are accessible here.
Relatively rare. Watch for taxi overcharging at Tbilisi airport. Use Bolt or Yandex Go.
Very nomad-friendly. No income tax for foreign-sourced income in many cases. VPN use is legal.
e-Visa required for many nationalities. Check eligibility at evisa.gov.tr. Stays up to 90 days.
Turkcell, Vodafone Turkey, Türk Telekom. Tourist SIMs must be registered — bring your passport.
Medium risk. Turkey blocks some social media platforms intermittently. VPN essential.
Common: Istanbul carpet shop pressure sales, shoe shine drop scam, taxi meter fraud.
VPN use is in a legal gray area — technically restricted but widely used. Social media criticism of the government carries legal risk.
Schengen zone. Digital Nomad Visa available for non-EU remote workers (up to 12 months, renewable).
Cosmote, Vodafone Greece, Wind Hellas. Good coverage on the mainland, spottier on smaller islands.
Low risk. Public WiFi available in most cafés and tourist areas. Use VPN as standard.
Common: Athens taxi overcharging, fake ticket sellers for Acropolis, bar scams targeting solo travelers.
VPN use is legal. Photography of military sites is prohibited. Greece has one of Europe's more affordable nomad visa programs.
Schengen zone. Denmark does not currently offer a dedicated digital nomad visa.
TDC, Telenor Denmark, Telia. Excellent coverage and fast speeds. Prepaid SIMs at 7-Eleven.
Very low risk. Strong privacy culture. Public WiFi widely available and generally secure.
Very rare. Watch for overpriced tourist restaurants near Nyhavn and Tivoli.
VPN use is fully legal. Denmark is one of the safest countries for digital nomads overall.
Visa exemption for many nationalities (30–60 days). LTR Visa (Long-Term Resident) available for remote workers.
AIS, DTAC (now merged with True), True Move. Tourist SIMs cheap and fast at the airport.
Low to medium. Some websites blocked. VPN recommended — widely used without legal issues in practice.
Common: tuk-tuk gem scam, temple closed scam, jet ski damage scam in Phuket, taxi meter refusals.
Lèse-majesté law — criticism of the monarchy carries serious criminal penalties. VPN use is common but technically restricted.
E-visa available for most nationalities (90 days, single or multiple entry). Visa on arrival available at select airports.
Viettel, Mobifone, Vinaphone. Fast and cheap data. SIMs require passport registration.
Medium risk. Government monitors internet activity. Facebook and Google are accessible but surveilled. Use VPN.
Common: motorbike taxi overcharging, fake travel agencies, cyclo tour bait-and-switch pricing.
Cybersecurity Law requires data localization for some services. Criticism of the government online carries legal risk. VPN use is common.
e-Visa available for most nationalities (30 days). Visa on arrival also available at major entry points.
Cellcard, Smart, Metfone. Cheap data. SIMs available at the airport without strict registration.
Medium risk. Government internet surveillance increasing. Use VPN. Some services blocked.
Common: tuk-tuk overcharging, fake NGO donation collectors, rigged casino games targeting tourists.
Avoid any criticism of the government or royal family online. Scam compound operations in border regions — stay away from unsolicited job offers.
Visa-free for most nationalities (90 days). K-ETA required for some countries. Digital Nomad Visa (Workation Visa) launched in 2023.
SK Telecom, KT, LG U+. World-class speeds. Pocket WiFi rental popular at Incheon airport.
Low risk. Some adult content and gambling sites are blocked. VPN works without issues.
Very rare. Watch for overpriced tourist restaurants in Myeongdong and Insadong areas.
VPN use is legal. Online criticism of North Korea is expected — criticism of South Korean institutions carries fewer risks. Photography near military bases restricted.
